title: eruca vesicaria subvar. hispida
intro: Eruca vesicaria subvar. hispida, also known as the hairy rocket or wild rocket, is a subspecies of the Eruca vesicaria plant, a member of the Brassicaceae family. It is a leafy green herb used for its medicinal and culinary properties, with a slightly bitter taste and crunchy texture. Traditionally, Eruca vesicaria subvar. hispida has been used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also consumed raw in salads or as a garnish, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function.
